Rubus fruticosus, commonly known as Blackberry, is a type of flowering shrub in the rose family. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Blackberry include anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Blackberry is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and jams, as well as in skincare products and supplements.
